name: Sustraia Trail
name_original: Miradores de la bahía de Zarauz — circular por el biotopo de Iñurritza
description: Jog this 2.4-mile moderate route through the Iñurritza Biotope, enjoying coastal views and the Bay of Zarauz.
region_intro: Jogging around Zarautz offers diverse landscapes, from the extensive sandy beach and its promenade to rolling hills covered in Txakoli vineyards. The region features a unique biotope with an estuary and sand dunes, alongside scenic coastal paths connecting to nearby towns. Runners can experience both flat, paved surfaces and more challenging trail runs with varied terrain and elevation changes. Best jogging routes around Zarautz The most popular jogging route is Getaria from Zarauz — circular trailrunning along the Camino del Norte, an 8.2 miles (13.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 34 minutes to complete, offering coastal views along the historic Camino del Norte. Another top favourite among local runners is Zarautz Beach Promenade – Zarautz Beach loop from San Pelaio, a moderate 8.7 miles (14.0 km) path. This route provides continuous sea views along the waterfront promenade and the beach. Local runners also love the Sustraia Trail, a 2.4 miles (3.9 km) trail leading through Txakoli vineyards and rural landscapes, often completed in about 28 minutes. Jogging around Zarautz is defined by sandy beaches, coastal promenades, and vineyard-covered hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy flat runs to more demanding trail running. The routes in Zarautz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 2000 runners have used komoot to explore Zarautz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Zarautz?
A: Zarautz offers a wide variety of running experiences, with over 35 routes available for you to explore. These range from easy coastal paths to more challenging trail runs through vineyards and hills.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in Zarautz?
A: Yes, Zarautz has several moderate routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed run. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38276264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palacio de Narros – Zarautz Beach loop from Zarautz&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.5 km path that offers a pleasant experience without significant elevation changes.

Q: What kind of scenic views can I expect on Zarautz's running trails?
A: Zarautz's running trails offer diverse and stunning scenery. You can enjoy continuous sea views along the extensive beach and promenade, explore unique ecosystems in the Iñurritza Biotope, or run through rolling hills adorned with Txakoli vineyards. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058586193/getaria-from-zarauz-circular-trailrunning-along-the-camino-del-norte" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Getaria from Zarauz — circular trailrunning along the Camino del Norte&lt;/a&gt; provide breathtaking ocean vistas.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Zarautz?
A: Absolutely. Many of Zarautz's running paths are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058586193/getaria-from-zarauz-circular-trailrunning-along-the-camino-del-norte" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Getaria from Zarauz — circular trailrunning along the Camino del Norte&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.2-mile loop offering coastal views.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Zarautz?
A: The running routes in Zarautz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Runners frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from the beautiful beach and promenade to the scenic coastal paths and vineyard-covered hills, offering options for all ability levels.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by historical or natural landmarks?
A: Yes, several routes offer opportunities to see interesting landmarks. For instance, the coastal path between Zarautz and Getaria often passes by historical whale-watching posts and offers views of the Mollarri mineral loading port. You can also explore highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4503659" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zarautz Beach&lt;/a&gt; or the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/852732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algorri Flysch&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1101604" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zumaia Flysch Cliffs&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any running trails that go through vineyards?
A: Yes, Zarautz is surrounded by hills adorned with Txakoli vineyards, providing unique running experiences. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1059004355/sustraia-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sustraia Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a 2.4-mile route that leads directly through these picturesque vineyards and rural landscapes.

Q: What is the Iñurritza Biotope and can I run there?
A: The Iñurritza Biotope is a unique protected ecosystem at the eastern end of Zarautz's beach, featuring an estuary, sand dunes, and marshlands. You can explore a moderate 2.4-mile route through this biotope, enjoying coastal views and observing diverse flora and fauna. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33907037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zarautz Beach Promenade – Zarautz Beach loop from Iñurritzako Biotopoa&lt;/a&gt; is a great option to experience this area.

Q: Are there any routes that connect Zarautz to nearby towns?
A: Yes, a highly scenic waterfront run connects Zarautz to the nearby town of Getaria. This route offers fantastic views of the Bay of Biscay and allows you to experience breathtaking ocean vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058586193/getaria-from-zarauz-circular-trailrunning-along-the-camino-del-norte" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Getaria from Zarauz — circular trailrunning along the Camino del Norte&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent way to experience this connection.

Q: What are the options for running along the beach or promenade?
A: Zarautz boasts the longest sandy beach in the Spanish Basque Country, stretching for 2.5 kilometers. Its waterfront promenade, 'El Malecón,' offers an ideal, mostly paved surface for joggers with continuous sea views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6974993" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zarautz Beach Promenade – Zarautz Beach loop from San Pelaio&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 14 km path that provides continuous sea views along both the promenade and the beach itself.

Q: Are there more challenging trail running options in Zarautz?
A: For those seeking more demanding runs, Zarautz offers challenging trail options with significant elevation changes. Climbing Talaimendi hill is highly recommended for spectacular cliff-top views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058586193/getaria-from-zarauz-circular-trailrunning-along-the-camino-del-norte" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Getaria from Zarauz — circular trailrunning along the Camino del Norte&lt;/a&gt; is classified as difficult and provides a good challenge with varied terrain.

Q: Can I find routes with good viewpoints for sunsets?
A: Yes, for spectacular cliff-top views and stunning sunsets, climbing Talaimendi hill is highly recommended. This vantage point, historically used to spot whales, offers rewarding vistas over the coast and the Bay of Biscay.
tips: From this viewpoint of Zarautz you will have a different view of the city. Here at the viewpoint you can also see the Church of Santa Bárbara as well as different vineyards that are famous for their txakoli. As you walk the path to the viewpoint, you will be able to observe an infinity of impressive landscapes and vegetation. Another essential on the road.
Beautiful views of the surrounding mountains of the town of Zarauz. Green hills and hills covered with vineyards.
One of the best areas for sunset

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The route features a varied terrain, predominantly consisting of paved surfaces. You'll encounter sections of street, hiking path, footpath, and some gravel and unpaved areas. The biotope itself is generally flat, making for a diverse but manageable run.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog this route?
faqs.answer: For jogging, this route is estimated to take approximately 28 minutes to complete, covering a distance of about 3.9 kilometers.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This circular route offers picturesque views, including the View of Zarautz Bay and the View of the vineyards and mountains of Zarautz. You'll also pass through the Iñurritza Biotope, a protected natural area with dune landscapes, sea cliffs, and an estuary. Don't miss the Sunset on the beach at Zarautz if you time your visit right.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners or those with lower fitness levels?
faqs.answer: While rated as 'moderate' for jogging, requiring 'good fitness', the route is considered 'easy' for walkers or hikers, suitable for 'any fitness level' and 'all skill levels'. If you're new to jogging, you might find it a good challenge, but it's very accessible for a leisurely walk.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I expect to see in the Iñurritza Biotope?
faqs.answer: The Iñurritza Biotope is rich in flora and fauna. You can observe various bird species, reptiles, amphibians, and insects, especially from the dedicated walkway. It's also an important stopping point for migrating birds, making it a great spot for nature enthusiasts.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Iñurritza Biotope is generally dog-friendly. However, as it's a protected natural area, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times to protect the delicate ecosystem and wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Iñurritzako Biotopoa?
faqs.answer: No, there are no entrance fees or permits required to access the Iñurritzako Biotopoa. It is a publicly accessible natural area, though visitors are encouraged to respect the environment and stay on marked paths.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Iñurritza Biotope?
faqs.answer: The biotope can be enjoyed year-round. Spring and autumn are particularly good for birdwatching due to migration. Summer offers pleasant coastal weather, while winter provides a quieter experience. The diverse scenery remains beautiful in all seasons.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Sustraia ibilbidea, the Northern Route (Basque Country), and the E1 Altxonbide ibilbidea: Zarautz - Bidegoian, among others.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: While specific trailhead parking isn't detailed, you can typically find parking within Zarautz town, which provides easy access to the biotope and the start of this circular route. Consider parking near the beach or town center and walking to the biotope entrance.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or facilities near the trail?
faqs.answer: As the trail is a circular route through the biotope and near Zarautz, you'll find numerous cafes, restaurants, and other facilities within the town itself, especially along the beachfront and in the town center, which are easily accessible from the trail.

waypoints.name: View of the vineyards and mountains of Zarautz
Sunset on the beach at Zarautz
